title: Auto-review applies require a complete requested harness batch
context: A live auto-review run judged only 5 of 21 drafts after per-draft provider failures, but the command hid those failures and --apply would still exit successfully after executing the partial plan. Because accept/delete is destructive review-queue state, missing requested judgments must be visible and must prevent mutation.
decision: When public auto-review requests subscription-CLI judgments, dry-run reports exact judgment coverage and bounded per-draft failures, while --apply refuses the entire batch unless every draft received a verdict. The explicit --no-llm --apply path remains available for intentionally deterministic-only dedup/grounding triage, and private review remains local.
consequences: A provider outage or unparseable response cannot produce a false-success partial apply.
Incomplete dry-runs remain inspectable and leave every draft unchanged.
Operators can explicitly select deterministic-only mutation with --no-llm instead of having that mode inferred from failure.
alternatives_rejected: Keep silently degrading failed judgments to keep-for-human during --apply — makes a partial evidence set look complete and can delete other drafts in the same run.
Abort incomplete dry-runs — unnecessary because dry-run is read-only and the partial evidence is useful diagnostics.
Retry indefinitely until every provider call succeeds — unbounded subscription use and latency, with no guarantee of recovery.
